name: Mirador de los Buitres, Os Fils y Castillo de Marcuello – circular route
name_original: Mirador de los Buitres, Os Fils y Castillo de Marcuello – ruta circular
region_intro: Hiking around Loarre offers diverse landscapes at the edge of the Sierra de Guara mountain range. The region is characterized by prominent limestone promontories, expansive agricultural plains of the Hoya de Huesca, and dramatic red conglomerate cliffs like the Mallos de Riglos. Trails often provide panoramic views of the Pyrenees and access to historical sites. Best hiking trails around Loarre The most popular hiking route is Castillo de Loarre - Pico Puchilibro via Cresta de los Ovnis (PR-HU 105), a 5.0 miles (8.0 km) trail that takes 3 hours to complete. This route features significant elevation gain and offers sweeping views towards the Pyrenees. Another top favourite among local hikers is Mirador de los Buitres, Os Fils y Castillo de Marcuello – circular route, a moderate 8.6 miles (13.8 km) path. This trail leads to a vulture viewpoint and passes by historical castle ruins. Local hikers also love the Loarre Castle – Loarre Castle loop from Loarre, a 6.5 miles (10.4 km) trail leading through varied terrain with views of the castle and surrounding plains, often completed in about 3 hours 35 minutes. Hiking around Loarre is defined by limestone cliffs, the foothills of the Sierra de Guara, and the agricultural plains of the Hoya de Huesca. The network offers options for easy walks around historical sites to more challenging treks with significant elevation gain. The routes in Loarre are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 300 reviews. More than 1700 hikers have used komoot to explore Loarre's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes around Loarre?
A: Hiking around Loarre offers a diverse landscape. You'll find prominent limestone promontories, expansive agricultural plains of the Hoya de Huesca, and dramatic red conglomerate cliffs like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/349405" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mallos de Riglos&lt;/a&gt;. Trails range from easy paths around historical sites to more challenging treks with significant elevation gain in the foothills of the Sierra de Guara.

Q: Are there any easy or family-friendly hiking trails in Loarre?
A: Yes, Loarre offers 4 easy hiking routes suitable for families or those looking for a relaxed walk. Many of these are shorter loops around the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3983714" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Loarre Castle&lt;/a&gt;, providing historical insights and scenic views without significant elevation.

Q: What are some of the more challenging hikes in the Loarre area?
A: For experienced trekkers, Loarre features 4 difficult routes. A notable challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17546180" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Loarre Castle – Loarre Castle loop from Loarre&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 6.5 miles (10.4 km) with over 670 meters of elevation gain. Another demanding trail is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e943427833/castillo-de-loarre-pico-puchilibro-via-cresta-de-los-ovnis-pr-hu-105" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Loarre - Pico Puchilibro via Cresta de los Ovnis (PR-HU 105)&lt;/a&gt;, offering sweeping views towards the Pyrenees.

Q: What historical sites or natural landmarks can I see while hiking in Loarre?
A: Loarre is rich in both history and natural beauty. The magnificent &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3983714" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Loarre&lt;/a&gt; is a central feature, with many trails offering views of or access to it. You can also explore the dramatic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/349405" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mallos de Riglos&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2703458" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mallos de Agüero&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/793375" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Marcuello Castle&lt;/a&gt; ruins. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e937652589/mirador-de-los-buitres-os-fils-y-castillo-de-marcuello-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de los Buitres&lt;/a&gt; offers a chance to spot griffon vultures.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes around Loarre?
A: Yes, many routes in Loarre are designed as loops. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e937652589/mirador-de-los-buitres-os-fils-y-castillo-de-marcuello-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de los Buitres, Os Fils y Castillo de Marcuello – circular route&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you past a vulture viewpoint and historical castle ruins. Another well-liked loop is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10465777" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Loarre Castle – Loarre Castle loop from Loarre&lt;/a&gt;, offering varied terrain and castle views.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Loarre?
A: The hiking trails in Loarre are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.6 stars from over 300 reviews. Hikers often praise the stunning panoramic views, especially towards the Pyrenees, and the unique blend of historical sites like the castle with the dramatic natural landscapes.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Loarre?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on many hiking trails in the Loarre region. However, it's always recommended to keep them on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or near agricultural land. Be mindful of local regulations, particularly around protected natural areas or historical sites.

Q: Are there good viewpoints along the hiking trails in Loarre?
A: Absolutely. Loarre is known for its spectacular viewpoints. From the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3983714" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Loarre Castle&lt;/a&gt; itself, you get panoramic views of the Hoya de Huesca plains. Trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e943427833/castillo-de-loarre-pico-puchilibro-via-cresta-de-los-ovnis-pr-hu-105" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Loarre - Pico Puchilibro via Cresta de los Ovnis&lt;/a&gt; offer sweeping vistas towards the Pyrenees. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/324002" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Riglos Griffon Vulture Viewing Area&lt;/a&gt; is another popular spot for impressive sights.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Loarre?
A: The best time to hike in Loarre is typically during spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October) when the weather is mild and the landscapes are vibrant. Summer can be very hot, especially at midday, while winter offers a different charm but some higher trails might be affected by snow or colder temperatures.

Q: Are there other outdoor activities available in Loarre besides hiking?
A: Yes, Loarre is a hub for various adventure sports. The area is popular for canyoning and ravine descent in the Sierra de Guara, climbing at the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/349405" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mallos de Riglos&lt;/a&gt;, and paragliding due to favorable thermals. You can also find opportunities for mountain biking, horseback riding, rafting on the Rio Gállego, and even geocaching around the castle.

Q: Is there parking available for hikers near Loarre Castle?
A: Yes, there is parking available near Loarre Castle, which serves as a common starting point for many hiking trails in the area. It's advisable to arrive early during peak season to secure a spot, especially if you plan to hike directly from the castle.

Q: How many hiking trails are there in Loarre?
A: There are 17 hiking routes available around Loarre, catering to various skill levels. This includes 4 easy, 9 moderate, and 4 difficult trails, ensuring options for every type of hiker.
tips: One of the best points to observe the Mallos de Riglos, Peña Rueba and the Mallos de Agüero, which turn an even more intense red at sunset. It is also a very good place for observing griffon vultures.

A short distance from Marcuello Castle, the 12th-century Romanesque chapel of San Miguel was not the castle's chapel, as the castle had another one within its grounds. It was probably the parish chapel of a village that no longer exists, and what remains of it is this church, which is believed to be the only stone building.

The Marcuello castle is located on top, between the Loarre castle and the Riglos mallos, and offers spectacular views over La Galliguera and its imposing mallos. The first long route that I did alone on a bicycle took me to this place and at that moment, when I put the bike on the ground and lay down to enjoy the silence, I realized that from that moment on the mountain bike was going to be a fundamental pillar in my life. And it is that, sometimes, we need to run away to find ourselves.
